title: Container for a mixing system, mixing system for parenteral nutrition, and method for mixing
abstract: Container for a mixing system being filled with liquid parenteral nutrition and comprising a male connector and a female connector. The male connector is placed on the opposite side of the container compared to the female connector and the male connector and the female connector are embodied to be mechanically engageable with each other and to engage a mechanical connection with a male connector or a female connector respectively of another container and to form a channel, which enables the passage of liquid. At least two containers form a modular system for parenteral nutrition.
claims:
Claims Claims : 1. Container (2, 2a-2d) for a mixing system, being filled with, preferably liquid, parenteral nutrition and comprising a first, preferably male, connector (5) and a second, preferably female, connector (6), wherein the first connector (5) is placed on the opposite side of the container (2, 2a-2d) compared to the second connector (6), wherein the first connector (5) and the second connector (6) are embodied to be mechanically engageable with each other. 2. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to the preceding claim, wherein the first connector and the second connector are embodied to engage a mechanical connection with a first connector or a second connector respectively of another container in order to form a channel, which enables the passage of liquid. 3. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the first connector is a male connector and the second connector is a female connector. 4. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and/or the second connector (5, 6) comprises/comprise a membrane (9, 9a-9c) , and wherein the first and/or the second connector (5, 6) has/have a membrane breaker insert (20) which opens the membrane (9, 9a-9c) , when the container (2, 2a-2d) and the further container (2, 2a-2d) are coupled, in particular screwed, together . 5. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to the preceding claim, wherein the membrane breaker insert (20) is placed in a duct (13) of the first connector (5), preferably wherein the membrane breaker insert (20) is placed between a membrane (9c) and a tamper-evident seal (29) . 6. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the container (2, 2a-2d) comprises a locking device for engaging a, preferably sealed, non- detachable connection with another container (2, 2a-2d) in order to form a pre-assembled mixing system. 7. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to the preceding claim, wherein the locking device (40) is embodied to engage the non-detachable connection before the channel (23) between the containers (2, 2a -2d), which enables the passage of liquid, is formed and/or wherein the container (2, 2a- 2d) is embodied to engage a non-detachably, in particular non-twistably, connection when the container (2, 2a-2d) is connected with another container (2, 2a-2d) . 8. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to any of the claims 5 to 7, wherein the locking device (40) is embodied to provide an optical and/or acoustical signal when the container (2, 2a-2d) is non-detachably connected with another container (2, 2a-2d) and/or (2, 2a-2d) is non- detachably locked with another container (2, 2a-2d) after forming of the channel (23) , which enables the passage of liquid . 9. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the container (2, 2a-2d) comprises a hanger (3) , preferably 

 wherein the hanger (3) is mounted in an area of the male or the female connector (5, 6) . 10. The container (2, 2a-2d) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the container (2, 2a-2d) is embodied as molded bottle, in particular as blow-molded plastic bottle. 11. Mixing system (1) for parenteral nutrition, comprising at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) , in particular at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) form a modular system, wherein a first container (2a-2d) of the at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) is filled with a first liquid for parenteral nutrition and a second container (2a-2d) of the at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) is filled with a second liquid for parenteral nutrition, wherein the mixing system (1) comprises a septum (32) which is pierceable by a spike or a needle and a hanger (3) for attaching the mixing system (1) to an infusion rack, wherein the first container (2a-2d) comprises a first, preferably male, connector (5) and the second container (2a-2d) comprises a second, preferably female, connector (6) which fit together, and wherein the first and the second container (2a-2d) can be coupled together by connecting the first connector (5) of the first container (2a-2d) to the second connector (6) of the second container (2a-2d) , thereby forming a channel (23) , which enables the passage of liquid and the mixing of the first and the second liquid. 12. The mixing (1) system according to the preceding claim, wherein the mixing system comprises at least three 

 containers (2, 2a-2d) , in particular, the first container comprising amino acids, the second container comprising glucose and/or a third container comprising fat. 13. The mixing (1) system according to any of the preceding claim, wherein the first container (2a-2d) additionally comprises a second, preferably female, connector (6) and the second container (2a-2d) additionally comprises a first, preferably male, connector (5) which fit together. 14. The mixing system (1) according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising an ampoule or a vial holder (15) being connectable to at least one of the containers (2, 2a-2d) and/or a cartridge (4) filled with an ingredient, in particular an ingredient embodied as a powder, preferably wherein the cartridge is connectable to the second, preferably female, connector (6) of one of the containers (2, 2a-2d) . 15. The mixing system (1) according to any of the preceding claims , wherein the at least two containers (2, 2a-2d) are pre-assembled and inserted into a secondary package (42), in particular a secondary package (42) with an oxygen diffusion barrier and/or wherein the first and/or the second connector (5, 6) of at least one of the containers (2, 2a-2d) are sealed with a detachable tamper-evident closure. 16. The mixing system (1) according to any of the preceding claims , wherein one container (2d) of the containers (2, 2a- 2d) comprises a housing embodied as bellows (34) and/or wherein the mixing system (1) comprises a membrane 

 breaker insert (20) which is embodied as a separate component, which can be placed between the male connector (5) and the female connector (6) . 17. The mixing system (1) according to any of the preceding claims , wherein the septum (32) is provided in the first and/or the second connector (5, 6) and/or wherein the septum (32) is provided with connection means which are connectable to the first and/or the second connector (5, 6) of the containers (2, 2a-2d) . 18. Method for mixing a liquid for parenteral nutrition, in particular by using a mixing system (1) and/or a container (2, 2â2d) according to any of the preceding claims, comprising the steps: connecting at least a first container (2, 2â2d) comprising a first liquid for parenteral nutrition to a second container (2, 2â2d) comprising a second liquid for parenteral nutrition, thereby forming a fluid connection between the first and the second container (2, 2â2d) ; mixing the first and the second liquid; inserting a spike or a needle into a septum (32) which seals the first or second container (2, 2a-2d) . 19. The method according to the preceding claim, wherein the first, the first and the second container (2, 2a-2d) are preassembled in a first step, thereby forming a mechanical non-detachable connection without a fluid connection, and wherein the fluid connection between the containers (2, 2a-2d) is performed in a second step. 20. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the fluid connection is enabled by twisting the containers (2, 2a-2d) against each other and/or 

 wherein the mixing is performed by agitating the connected containers (2, 2-2d) and/or wherein a third container (2, 2-2d) comprising a third liquid for parenteral nutrition is connected with said first or second container (2, 2-2d) , thereby forming a fluid connection with said first, second and third container (2, 2-2d) .
